world.name: Supply Chain Realignment Simulation
world.domain: Strategic supply chain sovereignty and industrial resilience
world.boundedDescription: A bounded Narrative World covering semiconductor capacity, strategic logistics, export controls, industrial subsidies, and resilience doctrine across allied and competitive technology corridors.
world.summary: The world starts from a resilient but globally interdependent supply network. Across 18 months, industrial policy, export restrictions, logistics shocks, and alliance-level sourcing decisions push the system from escalating stress into a structurally realigned regime.

events.label: T0
events.title: Baseline Distributed Supply Stability
events.description: Baseline environment with diversified suppliers, moderate logistics slack, and no formal decoupling doctrine.

events.label: B1
events.title: Rare Earth Shipping Delay Rumor
events.description: Early shipping-delay rumors surface concern around mineral chokepoints and supplier concentration.

events.label: B2
events.title: Allied Resilience Subsidy Framework
events.description: A coordinated subsidy framework introduces formal language around allied sourcing preference and manufacturing resilience.

events.label: B3
events.title: Port Throughput Disruption Leak
events.description: A leaked logistics assessment points to strategic port saturation and low substitution capacity during crisis periods.

events.label: B4
events.title: Advanced Packaging Controls Precedent
events.description: A new controls precedent pulls packaging and assembly capabilities into strategic governance review.

events.label: B5
events.title: Strategic Foundry Reservation Doctrine
events.description: Governments begin reserving foundry capacity for strategic sectors, changing expectations of neutral allocation.

events.label: B6
events.title: Supplier Security Review Expansion
events.description: Supplier security reviews widen from frontier chips to broader tooling and logistics components.

events.label: B7
events.title: Logistics Insurance Stress Event
events.description: Insurance pricing shifts reveal that shipping and warehousing are being modeled as persistent strategic risk nodes.

events.label: B8
events.title: Strategic Manufacturing Sovereignty Act
events.description: Legislation formalizes domestic-capacity mandates for strategic technologies and critical inputs.

events.label: B9
events.title: Alliance Procurement Preference Leak
events.description: A leaked procurement framework indicates future preference for alliance-aligned suppliers across strategic categories.

events.label: B10
events.title: Credit Analysts Add Resilience Premium
events.description: Credit models begin pricing resilience and geopolitical alignment directly into supplier quality assumptions.

events.label: B11
events.title: Substrate Capacity Shock
events.description: A substrate bottleneck reinforces concentration risk and exposes low substitution flexibility.

events.label: B12
events.title: Reciprocal Export Restriction Package
events.description: A reciprocal controls package widens separation across equipment, software, and supplier certification pathways.

events.label: B13
events.title: Strategic Warehousing Repricing
events.description: Warehousing and inventory coverage costs embed assumptions of long-duration structural fragmentation.

events.label: B14
events.title: Bloc-Aligned Supplier Allocation Code
events.description: A bloc-aligned supplier allocation code formalizes a durable realignment of strategic sourcing and capacity access.
